content_preview.title: Google Patches Fourth Chrome Zero-Day of 2026 as CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Update Within Two Weeks
content_preview.summary: Google patches CVE-2026-5281, a use-after-free in Chrome's Dawn WebGPU layer exploited in the wild, marking the fourth Chrome zero-day of 2026. CISA gives federal agencies until April 15 to update.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
Google pushed an emergency Chrome update on April 1, 2026, patching 21 security vulnerabilities including a high-severity zero-day that was already being exploited in targeted attacks. The flaw, tracked as [CVE-2026-5281](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/google-fixes-fourth-chrome-zero-day-exploited-in-attacks-in-2026/), is a use-after-free bug in Dawn, the open-source cross-platform implementation of the WebGPU standard that Chrome uses for graphics processing.

Use-after-free vul...

editor_notes.content_quality: Concise, technically accurate cybersecurity news piece with proper CVE details, timeline, and actionable update info.
editor_notes.source_verification: BleepingComputer: verified — CVE-2026-5281, Dawn use-after-free, 'exploit exists in the wild' quote, Chrome versions, all 4 2026 zero-days confirmed. The Hacker News: 403 (bot-blocked), CISA KEV claims cross-corroborated by BleepingComputer. SecurityWeek: 403 (bot-blocked), Vivaldi patch and researcher info consistent with BleepingComputer reporting.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All CVE numbers, Chrome versions, and zero-day timeline verified against BleepingComputer.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Appropriate urgency without sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: Fresh topic — no prior Chrome zero-day coverage in recent archive.
concerns: 2 of 3 sources returned 403, but primary source (BleepingComputer) corroborates all major claims
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid cybersecurity news with accurate technical details. Approved.
